title: Ageing handmade paper with coffee and tea
materials: strong brewed coffee (cold) or strong brewed black tea (cold)
sources: SYNTHESISED — coffee and tea ageing is a long-standing craft technique. No canonical pre-1928 source; the chemistry (tannins in tea, brown pigmentation in coffee) is food-science not craft history.
notes: Toning white paper to a warm cream or aged brown using cold-brewed coffee or strong tea. Simple, safe, no specialist chemicals. Covers: making a strong brew (let it cool completely), applying with a brush or sponge in one pass, not going back over wet areas (streaks), drying face-up on baking paper, pressing flat under a weight once dry, multi-coat approach for deeper colour (each coat adds tone but also makes the paper more fragile — two coats maximum for writing paper). Coffee gives a warmer brown; tea gives a cooler parchment yellow. Use cases: aged-look envelopes, background paper for papercutting, journal page inserts.
